title: Host.project_root's payload-cwd leg is dead in production — no caller ever passes the payload
evidence: CodexHost.project_root documents resolution leg 1 as 'the hook payload's cwd ... the harness's own signal' (_host.py:178-180), and hostapi.Host.project_root(payload=None) carries the same leg 'for hosts with no project-dir env var'. But every production caller goes through _hooklib.project_root(), which takes no arguments and calls get_host().project_root() with no payload (_hooklib.py:353); entry mains resolve root BEFORE read_input() is even called (e.g. pre-write.py:132 vs 125). A repo-wide grep shows the only payload-passing callers are in the orphaned test suite (.github/scripts/test_codex_adapter.py:282-309). On Codex the documented primary signal never fires; resolution always falls to `git rev-parse` from process cwd.
impact: The seam's designed-in signal is dead code with a docstring asserting it works — the tests exercise a path production never takes. If Codex's process cwd ever diverges from the payload cwd (subdirectory sessions, future Codex versions changing hook spawn cwd), the fallback silently resolves a different root than the design intends, with no test catching it because the tests pass the payload.
recommendation: Thread the parsed payload into root resolution (read_input() once, then project_root(payload)) for the hook entries, or delete the payload leg and its docstring claims so the seam's real contract (env var / rev-parse / cwd) is what is documented and tested.
acceptance_criteria: Either a production call path passes the hook payload into Host.project_root, or the payload parameter is removed from the seam
test_codex_adapter's project_root cases exercise the same call shape production uses
